stem: What is classified solely as a disinfectant?
rationale: Alcohol is one of the most widely used skin disinfectants.
quarantineReason: Flawed: alcohol (keyed) is also an antiseptic; ambiguous best answer.

stem: What structure in the foot is the main weight-bearing support during walking and running?
rationale: First metatarsal joint is the largest and strongest joint which acts with the other phalangeal joints in a hinge action.
quarantineReason: Ambiguous key (medial longitudinal arch is equally defensible).

stem: When giving cardiopulmonary resuscitation to a child, after establishing unresponsiveness and calling 911, what should be the next step?
choices: perform mouth-to-mouth breathing after opening the airway
rationale: After establishing unresponsiveness and calling 911, an athletic trainer should get the AED/defibrillator or send a second rescuer to get one.
quarantineReason: Ambiguous CPR sequence; CPR application specifics are not tested on the BOC exam.

stem: Which of the following statements is correct regarding this modality?
choices: The spray should be held at a 90 degree angle.
The spray should only be 2/3 inches from the injured muscle.
The patient should do active stretching while the spray is being applied.
The spray should cover the skin area 1/2 times.
rationale: You should spray the area in order to cover the skin area 1/2 times.

stem: This stretching technique would be best if a person is suffering from what?
rationale: Stretching and strengthening the Achilles complex should begin as soon as possible with an Achilles tendon strain.

stem: In the history section of a spine assessment, what would be considered an INAPPROPRIATE question to ask the patient?
choices: Did you hit someone with, or land directly on, the top of your head?
How do you prefer to sit?
Do you have radicular paraesthesia down the extremities?
Is there any pain in your buttocks or the back of the legs?
Do you have a low level of pain?
rationale: Using medical terms like “radicular paraesthesia” is inappropriate and could be difficult for the patient to understand when trying to create a patient history of an initial assessment.
quarantineReason: Flawed: radicular paresthesia (keyed as INAPPROPRIATE) is in fact an appropriate spine-history question.

stem: What is the purpose of using prewrap under adhesive tape?
choices: to decrease chaffing to the underlying skin
increases the tensile strength of adhesive tape
to act as a barrier in cases of sensitive skin
to extend the effectiveness of the adhesive tape application in controlling motion longer
to eliminate the need to use skin lubricant
rationale: Using prewrap under adhesive tape in cases of sensitive skin. Where maximum support is desired, it is ideal to tape directly over skin.
quarantineReason: Flawed: keyed answer (d, extends taping effectiveness) contradicts its own rationale (skin protection); choices a and c are both defensible — two correct answers on a single-select item.
